Redispatch 2.0 — German transmission grid 2025

Scenario grid_beta · 7723 buses · 12911 lines · 8760-h day-ahead workflow: copperplate dispatch → DC power flow → §13a redispatch LP → revised flows. Line limit 100 % of s_nom · RES Mindestfaktor ×10 · KWK ×5 · PSTs and HVDC free.

Methodology

Day-Ahead Redispatch 2.0 (German Redispatch 2.0 / §§ 13a–c EnWG, in force since 2021-10-01). Each of the 8760 hourly DA dispatch outcomes is checked against PTDF-based linear line flows. For every hour with at least one line at |f| > s_nom, the runner solves a cost-minimising redispatch LP that minimises Σ_g m_g · c_g · |Δp_g| with the regulatory Mindestfaktor: m = 10 for RES, m = 5 for KWK / CHP, m = 1 for everything else.

Decision variables: aggregate per (bus, carrier) ramps Δp± for every unit with |PTDF| ≥ 2 % on any binding line, plus free Δp on all 14 HVDC links (ALEGRO, NordLink, Baltic Cable, etc.) and 19 phase-shifting transformers (PSTs). The LP is solved by scipy.optimize.linprog(method="highs"). The top-40 most-overloaded lines per hour are constrained; structurally infeasible residuals are absorbed by slack variables at 500 €/MWh, representing TSO emergency action beyond DA-redispatch.

Post-redispatch flows are computed as f_post = f_DA + PTDF · Δp_net. The model intentionally idealises the day-ahead mechanism: there is no intraday market, no 15-min resolution, no N-1 contingency reserve, and no balancing-group bookkeeping. Real BNetzA 2024 redispatch volume was 30.3 TWh at 2.78 G€ — substantial deviations indicate either residual structural overload in the topology (under-rated 110 kV lines in radial regions) or unmodelled topology measures (PST swing limits, network switching) that real TSOs use under § 13(1).
